question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 25.00% on 2026-04-10 for the Metaculus question "Will Benjamin Netanyahu cease to be Prime Minister of Israel during 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP on Netanyahu ceasing to be PM will be strictly above 25.00% on April 10. The CP is currently exactly at 25.00% — right on the knife-edge. The strict inequality means the status quo (no change) resolves NO. Today's major news — Israel's parliament passing the budget — removes the risk of early elections and stabilizes Netanyahu's position through Sep/Oct 2026, providing marginal downward pressure on CP. The CP has shown a slight downward trend over 6 weeks (26%→25%) with 816 forecasters providing stability. Monte Carlo simulation using observed volatility gives ~42% under a pure random walk, but drops to 21-31% with downward drift from budget news. Manifold markets at 40-42% for the underlying question are higher than Metaculus's 25%, which could pull CP up, but the communities have been divergent for weeks without convergence. Overall, the slight lean is toward NO given the strict inequality threshold and marginally negative news flow.

factors.description: CP is exactly at the threshold (25.00%), and strict inequality means status quo resolves NO

Budget passed today (March 30) — Netanyahu avoids early elections, stabilizes his position through Sep/Oct 2026. Marginally negative for underlying question probability, likely pushes CP slightly down.
Slight downward drift in CP over 6 weeks (26% → 25%), suggesting gradual erosion in forecaster belief Netanyahu will leave office
Opposition fragmentation (Eisenkot rejects Bennett merger) reduces probability of Netanyahu losing elections, could push CP down
Manifold markets show 40-42% for underlying question, higher than Metaculus CP of 25%. If Metaculus forecasters converge upward, this could push CP above 25%
Polls still show Netanyahu's coalition losing (51-53 seats vs 59-67 for opposition), supporting at least current CP level
816 forecasters = high stability, large base is slow to move, CP likely to stay near current level
Monte Carlo simulation with observed volatility gives ~42% base rate for CP > 25%, dropping to 21-31% under downward drift scenarios

reasoning:
**Forecast: 40% probability** that the CP will be above 25.00% on April 10.

Key reasoning:
- **Status quo favors NO**: CP is exactly at 25.00%, and the strict inequality (must be *higher than* 25%) means no change = NO
- **Budget passage** (today, March 30): Netanyahu avoids early elections with 62-55 vote, stabilizing his position — marginal downward pressure on CP
- **Slight downward trend**: CP drifted from 26% → 25% over 6 weeks
- **Large forecaster base** (816): Makes CP slow-moving and sticky
- **Counterweight**: Manifold markets at 40-42% for the underlying question are significantly higher than Metaculus's 25%, suggesting some room for upward convergence — but communities have been divergent for weeks without closing the gap
- **Monte Carlo**: Pure random walk gives ~42% probability; incorporating downward drift drops it to 21-31%

The 40% estimate reflects genuine uncertainty given the CP sits exactly at the threshold, with marginal evidence favoring a slight lean toward NO.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/40966) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-10 15:32:16 is higher than 25.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 25.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-10 15:32:16, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
